|Harvest Peak Capital

Blood / Biohazard

Last Edited: Feb 28, 2026

Last Launch

Feb 9

Next Launch

—

Playbook Launches

3

Active Plays

1

Active Tasks

16

Playbook Details

Biohazard Response Protocol | PDF

Jan 15, 2026 at 9:30am MST

Assignment Info

Tasks will be assigned based on specialty routing rules when the playbook is launched.

Task Name Description Due Priority Specialty
Secure the SceneImmediately restrict access to the affected area — do not allow staff, residents, or maintenance to enter1 Hour
Contact Emergency ServicesCall 911 if there is an active medical emergency; wait for authorities to officially release the scene1 Hour
Notify Regional ManagerAlert property manager and regional leadership of the biohazard incident with scope details1 Hour
Establish Access PerimeterSet up physical barriers and entry log — isolate unit plus hallways, stairwells, and elevators used as travel paths1 Hour
Staff Safety BriefingBrief all on-site staff that untrained personnel must not enter — OSHA Bloodborne Pathogens Standard (29 CFR 1910.1030) applies3 Hours
Isolate HVAC ZoneShut down HVAC serving the affected area to prevent airborne contamination spread to other units3 Hours
Document Contamination ScopePhotograph affected surfaces and document impacted materials — carpet, subfloor, drywall, baseboards, furniture3 Hours
Contact Licensed Remediation CompanyEngage a licensed biohazard/trauma scene remediation firm — verify licensing, insurance, and proper disposal certifications6 Hours
Notify Insurance ProviderFile initial claim notification with biohazard coverage details and preliminary damage documentation1 Day
Resident CommunicationNotify affected residents using neutral language (e.g. 'professional remediation in progress') without sharing occupant details1 Day
Coordinate Temporary RelocationArrange alternative housing for displaced residents and communicate realistic re-entry timelines1 Day
Oversee Professional CleanupMonitor remediation progress — containment, removal of porous materials, EPA-registered disinfectants with verified dwell times3 Days
Waste Disposal VerificationConfirm all regulated medical waste has been disposed with proper manifests and chain-of-custody documentation3 Days
Post-Remediation Clearance TestingObtain professional clearance confirmation that the area is safe for re-occupancy5 Days
File Insurance ClaimSubmit complete claim with full documentation, remediation invoices, and disposal manifests1 Week
Close-out ReportCompile final incident report including timeline, OSHA compliance verification, costs, and policy recommendations2 Weeks
